name: Unpatched Servers
description: Critical servers running outdated software with known CVEs.
vulnerability: Missing security patches
kri: % systems with critical patches > 30 days old

name: Phishing Attacks
description: Employees targeted by phishing emails leading to credential theft or malware.
vulnerability: Lack of security awareness
kri: Phishing simulation click rate

name: Insider Threat
description: Malicious or negligent insider accessing or exfiltrating sensitive data.
vulnerability: Excessive privileges, lack of monitoring
kri: Number of DLP policy violations per month

name: Ransomware Attack
description: Ransomware encrypting critical business data and demanding payment.
vulnerability: Unpatched systems, phishing, weak backups
kri: Backup restoration test success rate

name: Data Breach
description: Unauthorized access to sensitive customer or business data.
vulnerability: Weak access controls, unencrypted data
treatment_plan: Encrypt data at rest and in transit
kri: Number of unauthorized data access attempts

name: Third-Party Risk
description: Security breach through a third-party vendor or supplier.
vulnerability: Insufficient vendor security assessment
treatment_plan: Limit vendor access to minimum necessary
kri: % of critical vendors with completed security assessments

name: Weak Passwords
description: Users using weak or reused passwords enabling credential attacks.
vulnerability: Weak password policy, no MFA
kri: % of accounts without MFA enabled

name: Inadequate Backups
description: Critical data loss due to insufficient or untested backup procedures.
vulnerability: No backup testing, single point of failure
kri: Days since last successful backup restoration test

name: Cloud/System Misconfiguration
description: Systems deployed with insecure default configurations exposing data or services.
vulnerability: Default credentials, open ports, public storage
kri: Number of critical misconfigurations found per scan

name: No Incident Response Plan
description: Organization lacks documented incident response procedures.
vulnerability: No IR plan, untrained staff
kri: Months since last IR tabletop exercise

name: Privilege Escalation
description: Attackers or insiders escalating from standard user to admin/root access.
vulnerability: Misconfigurations, unpatched systems, weak access controls
kri: Number of unauthorized privilege escalation attempts per month

name: Supply Chain Attack
description: Compromise through a trusted vendor's software update or service.
vulnerability: Trusted vendor relationships, auto-updates
kri: % of critical vendors with completed security assessments

name: Cloud Storage Exposure
description: Publicly accessible cloud storage (S3, Azure Blob) containing sensitive data.
vulnerability: Default public access, misconfigured permissions
kri: Number of publicly accessible storage resources

name: Shadow IT
description: Employees using unauthorized applications and services without IT knowledge.
vulnerability: Lack of approved alternatives, no discovery tools
kri: Number of unauthorized SaaS applications discovered per quarter

name: Denial of Service (DDoS)
description: Distributed denial of service attacks overwhelming systems and causing outages.
vulnerability: Insufficient capacity, no DDoS protection
kri: Number of DDoS incidents per year and mean time to mitigate

name: API Security Vulnerabilities
description: Exposed or poorly secured APIs allowing unauthorized data access or manipulation.
vulnerability: Broken authentication, excessive data exposure, injection
kri: Number of critical API vulnerabilities found per scan

name: Excessive Data Retention
description: Retaining personal or sensitive data beyond legal requirements, increasing breach impact.
vulnerability: No retention policy, no automated deletion
kri: Volume of data retained beyond policy requirements

name: BYOD Security
description: Personal devices accessing corporate data without adequate security controls.
threat_source: Lost/stolen devices, malware on personal devices
vulnerability: No MDM, no encryption, no remote wipe
kri: % of BYOD devices enrolled in MDM

name: Social Media Information Leakage
description: Employees inadvertently sharing sensitive information on social media platforms.
vulnerability: Lack of awareness, no social media policy
kri: Number of social media policy violations detected

name: Legacy System Vulnerabilities
description: End-of-life systems that no longer receive security patches.
vulnerability: No patches available, outdated protocols
kri: Number of EOL systems still in production

name: Cloud Compliance Drift
description: Cloud infrastructure drifts from compliance baselines (PCI, HIPAA, SOC 2) as teams make unauthorized changes or misconfigure resources during rapid development.
threat_source: Developers, automated deployment pipelines, configuration drift
vulnerability: No cloud posture management, IaC without compliance checks, manual change approvals bypassed
treatment_plan: Enforce infrastructure-as-code with mandatory security review
Deploy drift detection with automated remediation
kri: Number of compliance drift events detected per week

name: CI/CD Pipeline Compromise
description: Attackers compromise the CI/CD pipeline to inject malicious code, exfiltrate secrets, or tamper with build artifacts.
threat_source: External attackers, compromised credentials, supply chain actors
vulnerability: Over-privileged CI/CD service accounts, unverified third-party actions, secrets in pipeline variables
treatment_plan: Lock down CI/CD service accounts with least privilege
Pin third-party actions to specific commit hashes
Implement signed commits and artifact signing
kri: Number of unauthorized CI/CD pipeline changes detected per month

name: Container Escape
description: A compromised container breaks out of its isolation boundary to access the host operating system or other containers.
threat_source: External attacker exploiting container runtime vulnerability
vulnerability: Outdated container runtime, containers running as root, privileged containers, missing seccomp/AppArmor profiles
treatment_plan: Apply seccomp, AppArmor, and SELinux profiles
Scan images for vulnerabilities in CI/CD
kri: Number of containers running with elevated privileges

name: Serverless Function Abuse
description: Attackers exploit serverless functions through event injection, excessive permissions, or denial of service via resource exhaustion.
threat_source: External attacker, malicious input via event triggers
vulnerability: Over-permissioned IAM roles on functions, no input validation, no rate limiting, exposed function URLs
treatment_plan: Apply least privilege IAM roles per function
Configure function concurrency and rate limits
Use private endpoints for internal functions
kri: Number of anonymous or unauthorized function invocations per day

name: Multi-Cloud Security Complexity
description: Using multiple cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) creates inconsistent security policies, blind spots, and increased attack surface.
threat_source: Security gaps between cloud providers, misconfiguration, inconsistent access controls
vulnerability: Different security models per provider, no unified visibility, inconsistent IAM policies, unmanaged shadow resources
treatment_plan: Deploy unified CSPM across all cloud providers
Standardize IAM policies and naming conventions
kri: Number of security configuration gaps between cloud providers

name: AI Model Poisoning
description: Attackers inject malicious data into training datasets, causing the model to learn incorrect behaviors, backdoors, or biased outcomes.
threat_source: External attacker, compromised data supplier, malicious insider
vulnerability: No data provenance validation, untrusted training data sources, no integrity checks on training pipeline
treatment_plan: Validate data provenance and integrity for training datasets
Implement data sanitization and anomaly detection
Use differential privacy techniques during training
Conduct adversarial testing of trained models
Maintain strict access controls on training pipelines
kri: Number of anomalous training data samples detected

name: AI Data Leakage via Model Outputs
description: LLMs and generative AI models inadvertently expose training data, PII, or trade secrets in their outputs through prompt injection or extraction attacks.
threat_source: External attacker crafting extraction prompts, employees using public AI tools with sensitive data
vulnerability: No input/output filtering for AI systems, employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools, no DLP coverage
treatment_plan: Deploy AI gateway with input/output content filtering
Implement DLP policies for AI tools
Block upload of classified data to external AI services
Train employees on safe AI usage
Audit AI tool usage logs monthly
kri: Number of PII exposure events detected in AI tool logs per month

name: AI Hallucination-Driven Business Decisions
description: Decision-makers rely on AI-generated outputs containing hallucinations or factual errors, leading to incorrect business, compliance, or security decisions.
threat_source: AI model's tendency to fabricate information, poorly designed RAG systems
vulnerability: Over-reliance on AI outputs, no human-in-the-loop for critical decisions, no output validation
treatment_plan: Design RAG systems with source citations
Set confidence thresholds with automatic escalation
Provide user training on AI limitations
kri: Number of verified hallucination incidents per quarter

name: Adversarial AI Input Manipulation
description: Attackers craft adversarial inputs (subtle perturbations, prompt injection, jailbreak attempts) that cause AI systems to misclassify, bypass guardrails, or produce harmful outputs.
vulnerability: No adversarial robustness testing, insufficient input sanitization, lack of guardrail testing
treatment_plan: Deploy AI guardrails and content filters
Test models against adversarial attack libraries
Implement rate limiting and abuse detection
Use ensemble models for critical classification tasks
kri: Number of detected prompt injection or jailbreak attempts per day

name: Rogue AI Agent Actions
description: An autonomous AI agent with tool access takes unintended actions — deleting data, modifying configurations, approving transactions — due to misaligned instructions or insufficient guardrails.
threat_source: AI agent misalignment, tool misuse, escalation from indirect prompt injection
vulnerability: Over-permissioned agent tool access, no human approval for destructive actions, no agent behavior monitoring
treatment_plan: Apply least privilege to all agent tool access
Require human approval for high-impact actions (deletes, writes, financial)
Log and monitor all agent actions in real-time
Set agent action budgets (max actions per session)
Implement kill-switch mechanism for rogue agents
kri: Number of AI agent actions requiring manual rollback per month

name: Software Dependency Confusion
description: Internal package names are registered publicly by attackers, causing dependency managers to install malicious versions instead of private internal ones.
threat_source: External attacker registering typosquatted or internal package names on public registries
vulnerability: No private registry configuration, no package pinning, no integrity verification for dependencies
treatment_plan: Configure package managers to use private registries first
Reserve internal package names on public registries
Pin dependencies to specific versions with hash verification
Scan for dependency confusion vulnerabilities quarterly
kri: Number of packages without pinned versions or hash verification

name: Vendor Data Access Sprawl
description: Vendors accumulate excessive data access over time (orphaned accounts, over-broad API tokens, unused integrations) creating an expanding attack surface.
threat_source: Compromised vendor accounts, M&A creating access accumulations
vulnerability: No vendor access recertification process, no expiration on vendor accounts, no monitoring of vendor activity
treatment_plan: Set expiration dates on all vendor accounts
Scope API tokens to minimum required permissions
Monitor vendor account activity for anomalies
Automate vendor account deprovisioning on contract end
kri: Number of vendor accounts with access not reviewed in 90+ days

name: Open-Source Dependency Vulnerability
description: Critical applications depend on open-source libraries with known vulnerabilities, abandoned maintenance, or malicious maintainer takeovers.
threat_source: Attackers exploiting known CVEs, maintainer account compromise
vulnerability: No SBOM, no automated vulnerability scanning, transitive dependencies not tracked, stale libraries
treatment_plan: Generate and maintain SBOM for all applications
Deploy SCA tooling in CI/CD pipeline
Define SLAs for critical vulnerability remediation
Monitor for library abandonment and fork if necessary
kri: % of dependencies with known critical vulnerabilities past SLA window

name: Managed Service Provider Breach
description: An MSP or MSSP with privileged access to multiple client environments is breached, creating a cascading supply chain compromise.
threat_source: Nation-state actors targeting MSPs for broad access, criminal groups
vulnerability: MSP has excessive privileged access, no MFA on MSP administrative accounts, no monitoring of MSP activity
treatment_plan: Restrict MSP access to minimum necessary with JIT / PAM
Require MFA on all MSP administrative accounts
Monitor all MSP-accessed sessions for anomalies
Maintain break-glass procedures to revoke MSP access immediately
kri: Number of MSP accounts with privileged access not reviewed in 90+ days

name: Hardware Supply Chain Tampering
description: Counterfeit or tampered hardware components (servers, networking gear, IoT devices) enter the organization through compromised supply chains.
vulnerability: No hardware provenance verification, purchasing from unauthorized resellers, no inspection procedures
treatment_plan: Purchase hardware only from authorized distributors
Verify hardware provenance and chain of custody
Conduct physical inspection of delivered hardware
Test hardware firmware integrity before deployment
kri: Number of unauthorized hardware reseller purchases in last quarter
